channel.title: Machine-Learning on Vasileios Balntas (vbalnt)
channel.description: Recent content in Machine-Learning on Vasileios Balntas (vbalnt)

item.title: Building a Mini Chess Engine with AlphaZero-Style Self-Play
item.description:
Introduction

AlphaZero revolutionized game AI by learning chess, shogi, and Go entirely through self-play, with no human game data. This post explores building a simplified version of this approach for chess.

The AlphaZero Approach

Brief overview:

  • Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) guided by neural networks
  • Self-play reinforcement learning
  • Policy and value networks combined

Architecture

Neural Network Design

  • Input representation (board state)
  • Policy head (move probabilities)
  • Value head (position evaluation)

MCTS Integration

  • Selection
  • Expansion
  • Simulation
  • Backpropagation

Implementation

Key Components

  1. Chess environment
  2. Neural network model
  3. MCTS search
  4. Self-play loop
  5. Training pipeline

Simplifications

  • Smaller board representations
  • Reduced network capacity
  • Limited search depth

Results

Lessons Learned

Future Work

References

  • Silver, D., et al. (2017). Mastering Chess and Shogi by Self-Play with a General Reinforcement Learning Algorithm
